author: Sherong Zhang, State Key Laboratory of Hydraulic Engineering Intelligent Construction and Operation, Tianjin University
Zhengqiao Wu, China Water Resources Beifang Investigation, Design and Research Co. Ltd.
Shihang Zhang, State Key Laboratory of Hydraulic Engineering Intelligent Construction and Operation, Tianjin University

description.en: An all-in-one publication version of the Structural Sustainability Compliance Ontology, integrating the SSCO-Core, SSCO-BFI, and SSCO-SCC modules.

creator.value: Sherong Zhang, State Key Laboratory of Hydraulic Engineering Intelligent Construction and Operation, Tianjin University
Shihang Zhang, State Key Laboratory of Hydraulic Engineering Intelligent Construction and Operation, Tianjin University
Zhengqiao Wu, China Water Resources Beifang Investigation, Design and Research Co. Ltd.

contributor.value: Xiaohua Wang, State Key Laboratory of Hydraulic Engineering Intelligent Construction and Operation, Tianjin University
Chao Wang, State Key Laboratory of Hydraulic Engineering Intelligent Construction and Operation, Tianjin University
Chen Zhu, Power Construction Corporation of China
Zhiyong Jiang, China Water Resources Beifang Investigation, Design and Research Co. Ltd.

bibliographicCitation.value: Zhang, S.; Zhang, S.; Wu, Z. Structural Sustainability Compliance Ontology (SSCO), version 0.1.1, 2026.

comment.en: A sustainability indicator used to assess environmental performance.
A spatial structural context that may contain substructures and structural elements.
The result produced by a compliance check.
A life cycle stage covering product-related processes before construction.

source.value: ISO 21678:2020, indicators and benchmarks; ISO 21931-2:2019, benchmarks or levels of performance.

comment.en: A reference level or benchmark used to interpret an indicator or criterion.

source.value: UNEP/SETAC, Guidelines for Social Life Cycle Assessment of Products, stakeholder categories.

comment.en: A category of stakeholders used to scope and interpret social life-cycle assessment issues and indicators.
A control that defines working and non-working time patterns for task execution.

source.value: ISO 21931-1:2022, sustainability performance framework; ISO 21931-2:2019, environmental performance framework.

comment.en: Performance related to the environmental, social, or economic assessment of a construction work.
An analysis state representing the final condition of an analysis task or step.

source.value: ISO 16739-1:2024, IfcCostItem and related cost control concepts.

comment.en: A control that governs planned cost, budget, or cost-related constraints for a task or process.
A material resource used by a task or process.
A compliance result indicating that a valid assessment cannot be established from the available evidence.

source.value: ISO 21929-1:2011, framework for sustainability indicators and core indicators for buildings.

comment.en: An indicator used to assess a sustainability aspect across one or more life cycle stages.

source.value: UNEP/SETAC, Guidelines for Social Life Cycle Assessment of Products.

comment.en: A stakeholder category representing suppliers and other value-chain actors relevant to social life-cycle assessment.

source.value: ISO 15686-5:2017, life-cycle costing and period of analysis.

comment.en: A cost item occurring over a defined period of analysis and contributing to life-cycle costing.
An analysis model representing the idealized structural analysis formulation for a project.
A definition of material assigned to a structural element or item.
A classification system or taxonomy used by the project.

source.value: ISO 15392:2019, sustainability in buildings and civil engineering works; ISO 21929-1:2011, sustainability indicators framework.

comment.en: An aspect of sustainability relevant to the assessment of construction works, typically environmental, social, or economic.
An ordered step within an analysis model.
A measurable quantity used to operationalize an indicator and assess an objective.
A boundary condition or constraint applicable to an analysis step or structural item.

source.value: ISO 21931-1:2022, framework for assessing environmental, social and economic performance; ISO 21931-2:2019, framework for assessing environmental performance.

comment.en: Performance relevant to sustainability assessment and related technical-functional reference conditions.
A project context that contains structural, analysis, classification, objective, unit, and compliance-related information.
A project objective to be measured by one or more metrics.
A sustainability aspect related to social performance.
A task within a process.

source.value: UNEP/SETAC, Guidelines for Social Life Cycle Assessment of Products.

comment.en: A stakeholder category representing local communities affected by the product system or constructed asset.

source.value: ISO 10303-104:2000, finite element analysis result structures.

comment.en: A set of analysis results produced by an analysis model.

source.value: UNEP/SETAC, Guidelines for Social Life Cycle Assessment of Products.

comment.en: A stakeholder category representing workers or employees affected by the product system or constructed asset.
A structural element in the product model.
A criterion used to assess whether a compliance requirement is satisfied.

source.value: ISO 9000:2015, nonconformity; ISO 37301:2021, nonconformity and corrective action.

comment.en: A non-fulfilment of a requirement identified during a compliance check.
A reference that links a structural element to a classification entry.

source.value: ISO 15686-1:2011, life-cycle stages; ISO 15392:2019, life cycle of construction works.

comment.en: A stage in the life cycle of a construction work to which indicators or performances may relate.
A labor resource used by a task or process.
An initial or final analysis state associated with a task.
A specification that defines the default units used for quantitative values within a project context.
A life cycle stage covering use, operation, maintenance, and related service life processes.
A control that governs the planned timing and sequencing of tasks.
A result subset corresponding to a particular analysis step.
An analysis-level structural item idealized as a line-based representation.
An analysis-level structural item idealized as a surface-based representation.
A life cycle stage covering on-site construction and installation processes.
A control that governs task execution and may allocate resources.
A life cycle stage covering deconstruction, disposal, recovery, or recycling processes.
A compliance obligation that shall be satisfied and may be specified by indicators and criteria.
An analysis state representing the initial condition of an analysis task or step.
A sustainability indicator used to assess economic performance.
A construction or engineering process associated with a spatial or structural context.

source.value: ISO 16739-1:2024; ISO 10303-104:2000, finite element idealization structures.

comment.en: An analysis-level structural item idealized from a structural element.

source.value: ISO 15392:2019, technical and functional performance; ISO 21931-2:2019, technical and functional characteristics and functional equivalent.

comment.en: Technical and functional performance considered as a reference condition for sustainability assessment.

source.value: ISO 14044:2006, goal and scope definition; ISO 15686-5:2017, period of analysis for life-cycle costing.

comment.en: A goal-and-scope setting that states the functional unit, system boundary, study period, allocation method, cut-off rule, and data-quality context used for a life-cycle sustainability assessment.
A compliance result indicating that the checked criterion is only partly satisfied.

source.value: UNEP/SETAC, Guidelines for Social Life Cycle Assessment of Products.

comment.en: A stakeholder category representing society-level concerns relevant to social life-cycle assessment.
A sustainability aspect related to economic performance.
A generic resource consumed or allocated in a task.
A compliance result indicating that the checked criterion is not satisfied.
An equipment resource used by a task or process.
An activity that checks a criterion and produces a compliance result.
A quantified input, output, emission, waste, or other inventory flow associated with a task and used in life cycle inventory analysis.
A compliance result indicating that the checked criterion is satisfied.
An analysis-level structural item idealized as a solid-based representation.

source.value: UNEP/SETAC, Guidelines for Social Life Cycle Assessment of Products.

comment.en: A stakeholder category representing consumers or end users relevant to social life-cycle assessment.
A sustainability indicator used to assess social performance.
A definition describing an analysis state.
A sustainability aspect related to environmental performance.
Links a compliance criterion to a benchmark.
Links a results step to its analysis step.
Specifies the date associated with a reported performance value.
Specifies the ordinal position of a task or analysis step within a sequence.
Specifies an external identifier used to trace an instance back to a source model, document, or system.
Specifies the comparison operator used in a compliance criterion, such as <=, >=, =, <, or >.
Specifies the unit associated with a target value.
Specifies the name of the software used to create or run an analysis model.
Links a classification reference to a classification.
Links a spatial structure element to a structural element.
Specifies the planned duration value associated with a control.
Links a task to an inventory flow used in life cycle inventory analysis.
Specifies a code or notation assigned to a classification entry or system.
Links a task to an initial analysis state.
Specifies the cut-off rule adopted by the assessment scenario.
Specifies the data source, dataset, or provenance statement for an inventory flow.
Specifies the edition or version identifier of a classification system.
Links a project to a spatial structure element.
Specifies the number of work shifts defined by a calendar control.
Specifies the planned finish date and time associated with a control.
Specifies the number of working hours per day defined by a calendar control.
Specifies the baseline budget amount represented by a cost control.
Specifies the local unit of an embodied carbon factor assigned to a material resource when an explicit value-level unit needs to override or supplement the project unit specification.
Specifies the document in which a requirement or criterion is stated.
Specifies the name of a baseline schedule represented by a schedule control.
Specifies the method, procedure, or execution mode used for a task.
Links a structural item to the task for which it is analyzed.
Links a compliance result to a performance basis.
Specifies the type or category of a resource.
Links a control to a resource.
Specifies the scalar value of a benchmark used for interpretation or comparison.
Defines sequence among tasks.
Specifies the ratio of material waste associated with a material resource during a task or process.
Specifies the tensile strength value assigned to a material definition, interpreted by default through the project unit specification unless a local unit is explicitly provided.
Links a compliance check to a compliance result.
Specifies the name of a classification system.
Links a sustainability performance value to the inventory flow data from which it is derived.
Links a task to a control.
Specifies the technical or functional assessment method used to establish a functional or technical performance value.
Specifies an identifier assigned to a compliance criterion.
Relates a compliance criterion to the structural item to which it applies.
Specifies the local unit of a resource quantity when an explicit value-level unit needs to override or supplement the project unit specification.
Specifies the numeric quantity of the functional unit used by the assessment scenario.
Specifies the ratio of female workers within a labor resource.
Specifies the name or code of a load case associated with an analysis step.
Specifies the scalar value of a response variable reported in an analysis results step.
Specifies an uncertainty statement associated with an inventory flow.
Specifies an identifier assigned to a compliance requirement.
Links a project to an objective.
Specifies the location type at which a response variable is reported, such as nodal, elemental, or section-based.
Specifies the textual statement of the system boundary used by the assessment scenario.
Specifies the unit price of a resource.
Specifies the number of workers represented by a labor resource.
Specifies the type or category of an objective, such as sustainability, cost, schedule, or compliance.
Specifies the statistic type of a reported response value, such as maximum, minimum, average, or characteristic.
Specifies the earned value amount represented by a cost control.
Links a project to the assessment scenario that defines the goal-and-scope conditions of a life-cycle sustainability assessment.
Specifies the ratio of male workers within a labor resource.
Specifies the maximum displacement value represented in an analysis result set, interpreted by default through the project unit specification unless a local unit is explicitly provided.
Specifies the maximum stress value represented in an analysis result set, interpreted by default through the project unit specification unless a local unit is explicitly provided.
Specifies the local unit of a fuel consumption value associated with an equipment resource when an explicit value-level unit needs to override or supplement the project unit specification.
Specifies the name of the assessment method used to establish a sustainability performance value.
Relates a compliance check to the compliance criterion that it checks.
Specifies the edition or version associated with a classification reference.
Indicates whether a schedule control belongs to a critical path condition.
Links a life-cycle cost item to the resource to which the cost refers.
Specifies the fuel consumption amount associated with an equipment resource.
Specifies a textual description of non-working periods, holidays, or exceptions in a calendar control.
Links an indicator to a benchmark.
Specifies the location path, section, or hierarchical position of a classification reference within a classification system.
Specifies the degree of freedom to which a constraint applies.
Specifies the source from which a benchmark value is derived.
Specifies the local currency of a cost value associated with a resource when an explicit value-level currency needs to override or supplement the project unit specification.
Specifies the numeric embodied carbon factor of a material resource, interpreted by default through the project unit specification unless a local unit is explicitly provided.
Specifies the type of a structural element in the product model.
Specifies the grade or class of a material definition.
Specifies the unit associated with the functional unit quantity.
Specifies the status of a compliance result, such as compliant, noncompliant, partially compliant, or not assessable.
Links a performance to a life cycle stage.
Links a task, inventory flow, life-cycle cost item, or other assessment element to the life-cycle stage in which it occurs.
Specifies an identifier assigned to an analysis state.
Links a task to a resource it consumes.
Links a constraint to a structural item.
Specifies the embodied carbon factor assigned to a material resource.
Specifies the name of a material definition.
Specifies the ratio of an equipment resource that is deployed from local sources or local contractors.
Specifies the total float or schedule slack value associated with a schedule control.
Specifies the density value assigned to a material definition.
Specifies the local unit of a threshold value when an explicit value-level unit needs to override or supplement the project unit specification.
Specifies the URI or web identifier of a classification system.
Specifies the type of an analysis step.
Specifies the unit of a state variable value.
Specifies a textual description of a nonconformity.
Specifies an identifier assigned to an analysis step.
Specifies the value type of a metric, such as scalar, ratio, percentage, count, or boolean.
Links a sustainability indicator to a compliance requirement that it specifies.
Specifies the unit of a response variable value.
Links an objective to a metric.
Specifies the utilization rate of a resource, typically expressed as a ratio or percentage.
Specifies the discount rate adopted for life-cycle costing analysis.
Specifies the type or category of a process.
Specifies the date and time at which an analysis result set is generated or recorded.
Specifies the year in which a life-cycle cost item occurs within the period of analysis.
Specifies the numeric wage rate value of a labor resource, interpreted by default through the project unit specification unless a local wage time unit or local currency is explicitly provided.
Links an analysis model to a structural item.
Specifies the code of a classification reference assigned to an element.
Specifies the textual description of the functional unit or functional equivalent used by the assessment scenario.
Links a compliance requirement to a compliance criterion.
Links an indicator to a performance.
Specifies the carbon emission amount associated with a resource during a defined activity, allocation, or consumption period.
Specifies a code assigned to a metric.
Specifies the type of a life-cycle cost item, such as initial, operation, maintenance, replacement, or end-of-life cost.
Specifies the planned or recorded time used by a resource.
Links a social indicator to the stakeholder category that the indicator addresses.
Specifies the ratio of a labor resource recruited locally.
Specifies the cost variance value represented by a cost control.
Links a structural element to a process by which it is constructed.
Specifies the total cost associated with a resource.
Specifies an identifier assigned to a set of analysis results.
Specifies the status of a process.
Specifies a qualitative description of the data quality assumptions adopted by the assessment scenario.
Specifies the clause number or clause identifier from which a requirement or criterion is derived.

Specifies a code or short identifier for a life cycle stage.
